title: Aira trichoides
intro: Aira trichoides, also known as Hair-grass, is a perennial grass species native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems, fever, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Aira trichoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Aira trichoides is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion, and it is also used in combination with other herbs to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures.
taxonomy: Aira trichoides is classified in the family Poaceae, commonly known as grasses. This classification places it within the order Poales, which includes various plants with similar characteristics. The family Poaceae is a large and diverse group of plants, and Aira trichoides is one of the species within it. Aira trichoides is also classified as a species of the genus Aira, which consists of several species of grasses. Further classification of Aira trichoides can be found at the species, genus, and family levels within the kingdom Plantae.
